QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $705.20 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: U.S. equities extended their tech-led stealth rally on Thursday, with QQQ surging 1.51% to 705.20 and SPY gaining 0.71% to 736.60. A surprise April Non-Farm Payrolls print and sustained Iran de-escalation, with WTI sliding 1.45% to $94.30, fueled the optimism. The FOMC’s rate hold at 3.50–3.75% and productivity data reinforced the ‘soft landing’ narrative.
commodities_note: WTI fell to $94.30, now down 7.5% over the past week, as Hormuz ceasefire talks progress. Gold edged higher to $4,754.60, while the DXY softened 0.35% to 97.89. QQQ data carries an anomaly: price $705.20 is outside expected range — verify data source for contract rollover or genuine historic move. No anomalies on other benchmarks.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain steady but uninspiring. US10Y holds above the 4.20% threshold that would signal NIM relief, and MAS’s S$NEER stance extends the dual compression headwind. OCBC reports tonight with the cluster’s strongest YoY profit trajectory, but DBS and UOB are structurally capped. NIM stabilization in OCBC’s print is the key catalyst.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — OCBC outperforming on profit growth, but NIM compression persists cluster-wide.
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.35% keeps SGD funding costs elevated, and MAS maintained appreciation pace in April, leaving no near-term relief for DBS/UOB.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is uniformly extended, with 11 of 15 watchlist names on CAUTION as SOXX surged to 510.74 (+3.6%). Mega-cap AI names (AMD, MU, NVDA, TSM) are 15-70% above their 50-day averages, and volume is dwindling. The underlying demand signals (Blackwell Ultra, HBM scarcity, CoWoS bottlenecks) remain structural, but price has overshot, making pullbacks the only viable entry path. The cluster’s near-term risk is a profit-taking rotation.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but tactically overbought — AI demand super-cycle intact; entries require 10-20% corrections.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD, MU, and SK Hynix each posted 5-30% weekly gains despite anemic volume, pushing the cluster to extreme extension thresholds. Tower Semi’s earnings on May 13 will test the priced-for-perfection bar.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverges: GOOG and AMZN are extremely extended on AI capex optimism, while MSFT trades near its 50-day and PLTR sits below it. The OpenAI credit circularity overhang continues to weigh on MSFT’s Azure narrative, and PLTR’s defense AI momentum has stalled. The cluster’s aggregate extension blocks broad entry, but the split offers a potential rotation trade if GOOG/AMZN pull back and MSFT/PLTR stabilize.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex-driven names are overbought; MSFT/PLTR are the cleaner setups if technicals improve.
big_tech.key_development: GOOG’s EU regulatory overhang adds risk atop the +23% extension; MSFT’s voluntary buyout program signals cost optimization.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 15-20% from current levels following the sector-wide optics selloff reported on May 7.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks

interconnected.entry_note: Warrants scouting if AMZN $200B capex commentary clarifies ROI path; monitor after AMZN's next earnings.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz blockade threat persists despite de-escalation, with oil supply risk priced at a $5-10 per barrel premium.
US-China semiconductor supply chain uncertainty via Section 122 tariff surcharges and China retaliation, targeting AI chip exports.
Federal Reserve policy pause at 3.50-3.75% with no clarity on cuts, keeping US10Y above 4.20% and pressuring bank NIMs and growth valuations.
EU Digital Markets Act probes into Google (Alphabet) and potential antitrust actions that could impact BigTech ad and cloud revenue.
geopolitical.market_impact: WTI has retreated to $94.30, easing energy input costs, but tech supply chain jitters keep semis cluster elevated. US10Y at 4.35% sustains the SGD funding cost headwind for Singapore banks and makes high-PE semis vulnerable. The QQQ data anomaly (price $705.20) may reflect index reweighting, not a structural break. PLTR and WRD sit near support, while the rest are extended.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: April Non-Farm Payrolls came in above expectations, reinforcing the ‘higher for longer’ rate narrative. CoreWeave guidance miss triggered a -14.5% gap down. Google granted more time to remedy EU competition concerns.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries in extended semis or banks until meaningful pullbacks (10-20%) reset risk/reward. Monitor OCBC print for NIM trajectory and TSEM pre-earnings for a potential sell-the-news correction.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensively positioned with a heavy cash overlay
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 21 names are on CAUTION — 15 from hard extreme extension blocks. Only OCBC’s earnings tonight and a potential CPI print on May 12 could trigger meaningful re-ratings; until then, the watchlist is effectively frozen.

watch_for_entry.note: Sizing R:R 1.5:1; ADR delisting risk and 65x PE cap entry. Catalyst: China AV permit resumption. Wait for reclaim above $7.34 on volume.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Only after China resumes AV permit issuance: watch $7.23–7.29
